methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $39M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Liminal Insights is a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Barry Van Tassell and Shaurjo Biswas that has raised $38.9M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 comparable companies: 1 public (LNZA) and 8 private firms including Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $39M in total capital raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, yields $78M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $175M, with the cohort ranging from $110M to $1.8B. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in the sector; Watershed's $1.8B valuation represents an outlier relative to the tighter cluster of $110M–$200M.

The two methods triangulate to a range of $66M (floor) to $201M (ceiling), with a median of $126M. This midpoint reflects reasonable weight on both stage-based progression from recent capital and peer market benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of Watershed's outlier valuation, which materially widens the peer range, and the degree to which Liminal's commercial traction and IP differentiate it within the congested climate-tech funding environment.
